To help you manage this new tax effectively, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on our website. This tool will allow you to easily calculate the impact of the new tax on your net salary.

### Practical comparisons

- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker from Lugano with a net salary of 4,000 CHF per month will pay 120 CHF more per month. This could mean a reduction in purchasing power, but also an improvement in local healthcare services.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker from Mendrisio with a net salary of 6,000 CHF per month will pay 180 CHF more per month. This could influence monthly expenses, but it's important to consider the long-term benefits.

### Regulations and dates

The new tax was introduced with Regional Law No. 15 of 2022, which came into effect on January 1, 2023. The tax was set at 3% of net income to finance doctors' salaries and improve healthcare services in Lombardy.

## Conclusion

The new tax on cross-border workers represents a significant change in the tax situation of those who work in Lombardy and reside in Switzerland. It's important that cross-border workers are aware of this new tax and plan their finances accordingly. The Lombardy Region has the challenge of demonstrating that the funds collected will actually be used to improve local healthcare services.

> "The tax is a necessity to improve healthcare services, but it's fundamental that cross-border workers are informed and prepared." — A representative of the Lombardy Region

📊 Relevant data:
- Average tax amount: 90-150 CHF per month for a net salary of 3,000-5,000 CHF.
- Most affected Ticino municipalities: Lugano, Mendrisio, Chiasso.

💡 Tip: Use the salary calculator for an accurate estimate and plan your finances accordingly.

⚠️ Warning: Make sure to consult an accountant to avoid errors in your tax return.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/ora-e-vero-nuova-tassa-sui-frontalieri-in-svizzera-per-dare-10mila-euro-in-piu-ai-medici-e-5400-agli-infermieri/)*

item.title: Varese receives 35,000 euros to enhance its attractions
item.description: Lombardy Region funds territorial marketing: Varese obtains 35,000 euros to promote its cultural and tourist excellences.
item.content:encoded:
The Lombardy Region has allocated a total investment of 319,813 euros to support eight territorial marketing projects presented by the provincial capital municipalities. Among the beneficiaries is Varese, which will receive 35,000 euros to enhance its unique features. These funds, made available by the '2026 Territorial Promotion Line', have fully financed the municipalities' proposals, with the aim of promoting the economic, cultural, and tourist growth of the Lombard territory.

Territorial marketing has been identified as a fundamental lever to strengthen the attractiveness and visibility of the territories, especially in an increasingly competitive context. The regional tourism assessor, Debora Massari, emphasized the importance of using this tool to authentically and distinctively tell the identity of the places, networking cultural heritage, local traditions, craftsmanship, and gastronomic excellence.

### The financing of these projects also fits into the framework of the new 'tourist brand identity' of Lombardy, presented on April 15, 2026. The goal is to build a more recognizable and coherent image of the region, both online and offline, through targeted actions that strengthen the positioning of Lombard territories at national and international levels.

📊 **Concrete examples and real numbers**
Varese intends to use the 35,000 euros to promote its natural heritage, particularly the Sacro Monte, which attracts about 200,000 visitors a year. The project includes targeted digital campaigns, with a predicted 15% increase in foreign tourists by 2027. In comparison, Lugano, in Ticino, invested 50,000 Swiss francs in 2025 to promote its lake and museums, with a 20% increase in Italian visitors.

💡 Operational checklist for municipalities
- Territory analysis: Identify local resources (cultural, natural, gastronomic).
- Definition of objectives: Economic, tourist, cultural growth.
- Activity planning: Digital campaigns, events, collaborations with local entities.
- Monitoring and evaluation: Use of KPIs such as number of visitors, tourist revenues, social media engagement.

⚠️ Regulations and comparison with Ticino
In Lombardy, the '2026 Territorial Promotion Line' provides a maximum funding of 50,000 euros per project. In Ticino, the 2024 cantonal law on tourism provides contributions up to 100,000 francs for territorial marketing projects, with a priority for cross-border projects. For example, the municipality of Mendrisio received 75,000 francs to promote its historic center, with a focus on Lombard tourists.

> “Territorial marketing is a strategic investment for the growth of the territory,” declared assessor Massari.

📊 Comparison between practical scenarios
Varese aims to increase foreign tourists by 15% thanks to digital campaigns, while Mendrisio aims for a 10% increase in Italian visitors through cultural events. Both municipalities use historical data and market analysis to define their strategies, with particular attention to sustainability and innovation.

In conclusion, the investment by the Lombardy Region represents a significant step towards the enhancement of the territories, with a model that could be replicated in other regions, including neighboring Switzerland. Varese, with a grant of 35,000 euros, will have the opportunity to develop initiatives that promote its uniqueness and excellence. The city and its territory are rich in tourist, cultural, and historical potential, and regional support will further strengthen its presence on the national and international scene. The funded activities will contribute to enhancing Varese's image, promoting events, thematic reviews, and integrated campaigns that highlight its local identity and make it an increasingly attractive destination for tourists.

In addition to Varese, other provincial capitals of Lombardy have received significant contributions for their territorial marketing projects: Bergamo: 20,000 euros, Brescia: 48,983 euros, Cremona: 47,580 euros, Lodi: 43,000 euros, Mantova: 42,000 euros, Pavia: 41,250 euros, Sondrio: 42,000 euros. These funds are destined for projects that, like Varese's, aim to create attractive experiences for visitors, strengthening the valorization of local identities and generating lasting value for the communities.

### The Commitment of Regione Lombardia

With this measure, Regione Lombardia demonstrates a strong belief in the strength of the territories and their ability to network. Debora Massari has clarified: 'With this measure, we invest in projects that put local identities at the center and transform them into attractive experiences, capable of generating lasting value. Fully financing all the presented projects means recognizing this quality and accompanying it on a structured growth path.' The goal is to make Lombardy an excellence destination, capable of telling its story with authenticity, innovation, and ambition, consolidating its role both nationally and internationally.

### Concrete Examples and Real Numbers

Varese, for example, could use the 35,000 euros to organize events like the Garden Festival, which attracts over 100,000 visitors each year, or to promote the Sacred Mountain of Varese, a UNESCO heritage site. In comparison, Ticino invested 2.5 million Swiss francs in 2023 to promote cross-border tourism, with a focus on Lugano and Locarno. These funds allowed for the organization of events like the Blue Festival in Lugano, which saw the participation of over 50,000 people in 2023.

### Regulations and Amounts

In Lombardy, funds for territorial marketing are regulated by Regional Law No. 31 of 2014, which provides for contributions of up to 50,000 euros for tourist valorization projects. In Ticino, the Tourism Law of 2010 stipulates that contributions for territorial marketing cannot exceed 50% of the total project budget. This means that Ticino municipalities must co-finance at least 50% of the initiatives.

### Operational Checklist

For Lombard municipalities that want to access these funds, it is important to follow an operational checklist:
1. **Project Submission**: The project must be submitted by September 30 of each year.
2. Documentation: A detailed financial plan and technical report must be attached.
3. Evaluation: Projects are evaluated by a regional commission by November 30.
4. Approval: Funds are disbursed by December 31.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

In Lombardy, regional funds allow for full financing of projects, while in Ticino, municipalities must co-finance at least 50% of the initiatives. This means that a Lombard municipality like Varese can count on full funding of 35,000 euros, while a Ticino municipality like Lugano would have to co-finance at least 35,000 Swiss francs for a 70,000-franc project.

### Conclusions

The initiative of Regione Lombardia represents an important step forward in the valorization of territories and the promotion of tourism. With a total investment of over 300,000 euros, the provincial capitals of Lombardy will have the opportunity to develop projects that can attract visitors and generate lasting value for local communities. In comparison, Ticino has adopted a different approach, with a greater focus on co-financing and specific projects such as cross-border tourism. Both models, however, demonstrate the regions' commitment to promoting their excellence and strengthening their presence on the international scene.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Varese receives 35,000 euros to enhance its attractions

For cross-border workers living in Ticino and working in Lombardy, the 35,000 euro investment in Varese's territorial marketing could offer new opportunities for leisure and tourism. Enhancing Varese's attractions, such as the Sacro Monte and Villa Mirabello Park, could attract more visitors, including cross-border workers, who could discover new destinations for their free time.

### Opportunities for Ticino cross-border workers

Cross-border workers from Ticinese towns like Mendrisio, Chiasso, and Lugano who work in Varese could benefit from these initiatives. For example, Varese's Sacro Monte, a UNESCO World Heritage site, attracts around 100,000 visitors per year. With the new investment, a 20% increase in visitors is expected, offering new leisure opportunities for cross-border workers.

### Operational checklist for cross-border workers

1. **Monitor events**: Follow Varese's social media and official websites to stay updated on upcoming events and promotions.
2. Plan visits: Use our [cost calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to estimate your travel expenses.
3. Discover the highlights: Plan visits to Sacro Monte, Villa Mirabello Park, and other sites of interest.

### Practical comparisons

- Ticket cost: A ticket to Sacro Monte costs around 5 euros, while a ticket to Villa Mirabello Park is free.
- Travel time: It takes about 30 minutes by car from Mendrisio to Varese, and about 45 minutes from Lugano.

### Regulations and benefits

According to the Convention between the Swiss Confederation and the Italian Republic of December 23, 1974, cross-border workers in Italy must comply with specific tax regulations. However, territorial marketing initiatives do not directly influence these regulations but offer new leisure opportunities.

> "Varese is a city rich in history and culture, and with these investments, we can offer unique experiences to our visitors."

### Conclusion

If you are a cross-border worker and want to take advantage of Varese's new territorial marketing initiatives, we recommend monitoring upcoming events and promotions. Use our [cost calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to estimate your travel expenses and discover the best offers available. Don't miss the opportunity to explore Varese's attractions and live a unique and authentic experience.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/regione-lombardia-finanza-il-marketing-territoriale-varese-riceve-35-000-euro-per-valorizzare-le-sue-bellezze/2556304/)*

item.title: Canton and Municipalities United Against Climate Change
item.description: Symposium between Canton and Municipalities to address climate change. Gobbi: 'The 2023 flood in Vallemaggia demonstrated the need to act in advance'
item.content:encoded:
## Symposium for an alliance against climate change

Today, an important symposium was held between the Canton of Ticino and the municipalities to discuss joint strategies against climate change. The initiative was born in response to the extreme weather events of recent years, such as the 2023 flood in Vallemaggia, which demonstrated the need to act in advance. Councilor of State Norman Gobbi emphasized that not intervening has a cost, but more coordination is needed between the different institutional levels.

### The need for coordinated action

Gobbi highlighted that local authorities can do a lot, but it is essential to have an alliance between the Canton and the municipalities to maximize the impact of the measures adopted. The goal is to create a common action plan that can address the challenges posed by global warming, such as rising temperatures and extreme weather events.

The 2023 flood in Vallemaggia caused severe damage and highlighted the vulnerability of the Ticino territory to climate change. This event prompted the authorities to reflect on the importance of preventing and mitigating climate-related risks rather than intervening only after the damage has been done.

### The role of local authorities

Gaia, an organization active in promoting sustainability, emphasized that local authorities can do a lot to combat climate change. For example, they can promote the use of renewable energies, improve the energy efficiency of public buildings, and encourage sustainable mobility. However, to be truly effective, these initiatives must be coordinated at the cantonal and municipal levels.

## Implications for cross-border workers

The alliance between the Canton and the municipalities to address climate change will have a significant impact on cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. The measures adopted could influence various aspects of their daily lives, such as mobility, energy, and waste management.

### Sustainable mobility

One of the main objectives of the common action plan is to promote sustainable mobility. This could include the expansion of infrastructure for electric mobility, such as charging stations for electric vehicles, and the encouragement of the use of public and shared transport. For cross-border workers, this could mean easier and more convenient access to sustainable means of transport to move between their place of work and residence.

### Energy efficiency

Another important aspect is the energy efficiency of buildings. The Canton and the municipalities could adopt measures to improve the energy efficiency of public and private buildings, such as thermal insulation and the installation of more efficient heating and cooling systems. This could have a direct impact on cross-border workers living in accommodations with reduced energy consumption, resulting in savings on bills.

### Waste management

Waste management is another area where the alliance between the Canton and the municipalities could have a significant impact. New measures could be introduced to reduce waste production and promote recycling, such as the creation of differentiated collection centers and awareness campaigns. For cross-border workers, this could mean greater awareness and ease in managing their waste in a sustainable way.

## What to do now

For cross-border workers who wish to actively contribute to the fight against climate change, there are several concrete actions they can take. For example, they can adopt more sustainable lifestyles, such as reducing energy and water consumption, using sustainable means of transport, and participating in local environmental volunteering initiatives.

### Adopting Sustainable Lifestyles

Reducing energy and water consumption is an important step towards a more sustainable lifestyle. Cross-border workers can adopt simple habits, such as turning off lights when not needed, using energy-efficient appliances, and installing efficient heating and cooling systems. Additionally, they can reduce water consumption by adopting practices such as rainwater harvesting and installing water-saving devices.

### Using Sustainable Means of Transport

Using sustainable means of transport is another way to contribute to the fight against climate change. Cross-border workers can opt for public, shared, or electric transport to travel between their workplace and residence. Additionally, they can participate in carpooling programs or use bicycles for short trips.

### Participating in Local Environmental Volunteering Initiatives

Participating in local environmental volunteering initiatives is a concrete way to contribute to the sustainability of the territory. Cross-border workers can join local groups that organize activities such as cleaning, tree planting, or environmental awareness. Additionally, they can participate in events and conferences to stay updated on the latest news regarding climate change and sustainability.

## Conclusion

The alliance between the Canton and the municipalities to address climate change represents an important step towards a more sustainable future for Ticino. For cross-border workers, this initiative offers the opportunity to actively contribute to the fight against climate change by adopting more sustainable lifestyles and participating in local initiatives. Use our [environmental impact calculator](nav:calculator) to discover how you can reduce your ecological footprint and contribute to a greener Ticino. ## Analysis of Practical Implications

The alliance between the Canton and the Municipalities to address climate change will have several practical implications for cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. Let's look at some of the most relevant aspects.

### Impact on Mobility

One of the main objectives of the joint action plan is to promote sustainable mobility. This could include the expansion of infrastructure for electric mobility, such as charging stations for electric vehicles, and the encouragement of the use of public and shared transport. For cross-border workers, this could mean easier and more convenient access to sustainable transport to move between their workplace and residence.

### Impact on Energy Efficiency

Another important aspect is the energy efficiency of buildings. The Canton and the Municipalities could adopt measures to improve the energy efficiency of public and private buildings, such as thermal insulation and the installation of more efficient heating and cooling systems. This could have a direct impact on cross-border workers living in accommodations with reduced energy consumption, resulting in savings on bills.

### Impact on Waste Management

Waste management is another area where the alliance between the Canton and the Municipalities could have a significant impact. New measures could be introduced to reduce waste production and promote recycling, such as the creation of differentiated collection centers and awareness campaigns. For cross-border workers, this could mean greater awareness and ease in managing their waste sustainably.

### Impact on Local Policies

The alliance between the Canton and the Municipalities could also influence local policies in the fields of urban planning, transport, and land management. For example, new regulations could be introduced to promote the construction of low-impact buildings and the creation of urban green spaces. This could have a positive impact on the quality of life for cross-border workers, offering them a healthier and more sustainable environment in which to live and work.

## Comparison with the Previous Situation

To better understand the implications of the alliance between the Canton and the Municipalities, it is useful to compare the current situation with the previous one. Before this initiative, measures to address climate change were often fragmented and not coordinated among the different institutional levels. This made it difficult to achieve significant sustainability goals.

### Sustainable Mobility

Before the alliance, infrastructure for sustainable mobility was limited and not always accessible to cross-border workers. For example, charging stations for electric vehicles were few and often concentrated in urban areas. Moreover, public transport was not always efficient or convenient for those living in peripheral areas.

### Energy Efficiency

In terms of energy efficiency, the previous situation was characterized by a lack of coordination between the Canton and the Municipalities. The measures adopted were often isolated and not sufficient to achieve significant goals in terms of energy consumption reduction. This made it difficult for cross-border workers to benefit from accommodations with reduced energy consumption and lower bills.

### Waste Management

Waste management was another area where the lack of coordination between the Canton and municipalities made it difficult to achieve significant sustainability goals. The measures adopted were often fragmented and not always effective in promoting recycling and waste reduction. This made it difficult for cross-border workers to manage their waste in a sustainable and conscious manner.

## Future Scenarios

The alliance between the Canton and municipalities to address climate change paves the way for several future scenarios for cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. Let's look at some of the most likely scenarios.

### Scenario 1: Increase in sustainable mobility infrastructure

One of the most likely scenarios is an increase in sustainable mobility infrastructure. This could include the creation of new charging stations for electric vehicles, the expansion of public transport networks, and the promotion of shared transport. For cross-border workers, this could mean easier and more convenient access to sustainable means of transport to travel between their place of work and residence.

### Scenario 2: Improvement of energy efficiency in buildings

Another likely scenario is the improvement of energy efficiency in buildings. This could include the adoption of new regulations to promote the construction of low-impact buildings and the installation of more efficient heating and cooling systems. For cross-border workers, this could mean the possibility of living in accommodations with reduced energy consumption and lower bills.

### Scenario 3: Promotion of recycling and waste reduction

Another likely scenario is the promotion of recycling and waste reduction. This could include the creation of new differentiated waste collection centers and awareness campaigns to promote sustainable behaviors. For cross-border workers, this could mean greater awareness and ease in managing their waste in a sustainable manner.

## Conclusion

The alliance between the Canton and municipalities to address climate change represents an important step towards a more sustainable future for Ticino. For cross-border workers, this initiative offers the opportunity to actively contribute to the fight against climate change by adopting more sustainable lifestyles and participating in local initiatives. Use our [environmental impact calculator](nav:calculator) to discover how you can reduce your ecological footprint and contribute to a greener Ticino.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who want to actively contribute to the fight against climate change, there are several concrete actions they can take. Let's look at some of the most relevant initiatives.

### Adopt sustainable lifestyles

Reducing energy and water consumption is an important step towards a more sustainable lifestyle. Cross-border workers can adopt simple habits, such as turning off lights when not needed, using energy-efficient appliances, and installing efficient heating and cooling systems. Additionally, they can reduce water consumption by adopting practices such as rainwater harvesting and installing water-saving devices.

### Use sustainable means of transport

Using sustainable means of transport is another way to contribute to the fight against climate change. Cross-border workers can opt for public, shared, or electric transport to travel between their workplace and residence. Additionally, they can participate in carpooling programs or use bicycles for short trips.

### Participate in local environmental volunteering initiatives

Participating in local environmental volunteering initiatives is a concrete way to contribute to the sustainability of the territory. Cross-border workers can join local groups that organize cleaning activities, tree planting, or environmental awareness campaigns. Additionally, they can attend events and conferences to stay updated on the latest news regarding climate change and sustainability.

### Monitor local policies

Monitoring local policies on climate change is another way to actively contribute to the fight against climate change. Cross-border workers can participate in public consultations, provide feedback to local authorities, and stay updated on the latest news regarding sustainability. Additionally, they can support local initiatives that promote sustainability and reduce environmental impact.

## Step-by-step procedure

For cross-border workers who want to actively contribute to the fight against climate change, here is a step-by-step procedure to adopt more sustainable lifestyles.

### Step 1: Assess your environmental impact

The first step is to assess your environmental impact. Cross-border workers can use online tools such as the [environmental impact calculator](nav:calculator) to discover how they can reduce their ecological footprint. This tool provides a detailed assessment of their energy, water, and waste consumption, and suggests concrete actions to reduce environmental impact.

### Step 2: Adopt sustainable habits

The second step is to adopt sustainable habits. Cross-border workers can start with small actions, such as turning off lights when not needed, using energy-efficient appliances, and installing efficient heating and cooling systems. Additionally, they can reduce water consumption by adopting practices such as rainwater harvesting and installing water-saving devices.

### Step 3: Use sustainable means of transport

The third step is to use sustainable means of transport. Cross-border workers can opt for public, shared, or electric transport to travel between their workplace and residence. Additionally, they can participate in carpooling programs or use bicycles for short trips. Use our [transport comparator](nav:transport) to find the most sustainable options for your trips.

### Step 4: Participate in local environmental volunteering initiatives

The fourth step is to participate in local environmental volunteering initiatives. Cross-border workers can join local groups that organize cleaning activities, tree planting, or environmental awareness campaigns. Additionally, they can attend events and conferences to stay updated on the latest news regarding climate change and sustainability.

### Step 5: Monitor local policies

The fifth step is to monitor local policies on climate change. Cross-border workers can participate in public consultations, provide feedback to local authorities, and stay updated on the latest news regarding sustainability. Additionally, they can support local initiatives that promote sustainability and reduce environmental impact.

## Conclusion

The alliance between the Canton and the municipalities to address climate change represents an important step towards a more sustainable future for Ticino. For cross-border workers, this initiative offers the opportunity to actively contribute to the fight against climate change by adopting more sustainable lifestyles and participating in local initiatives. Use our [environmental impact calculator](nav:calculator) to discover how you can reduce your ecological footprint and contribute to a greener Ticino.

item.title: Crans-Montana: UFAS clarifies healthcare costs for Italian patients
item.description: The Federal Social Insurance Office explains the procedure for reimbursing medical expenses for Italian patients injured in Switzerland.
item.content:encoded:
## The Standardized Procedure

The Swiss Federal Social Insurance Office (UFAS) has clarified the procedure for managing healthcare costs for Italian patients injured in Switzerland, providing operational and regulatory details. According to current regulations, healthcare costs for insured individuals from an EU or EEA state who are injured in Switzerland are settled within the Confederation and provisionally reimbursed to the Swiss service provider, such as a hospital. For example, in 2022, medical expenses for Italian patients hospitalized in Ticino hospitals like the Civico in Lugano or the Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) amounted to approximately 5 million francs, later reimbursed to Italian insurers.

### Operational Checklist for Service Providers

- **Patient Identification**: Verify the patient's nationality and health insurance.
- Documentation: Collect all necessary medical and administrative documents.
- Provisional Billing: Issue the invoice to the Swiss service provider.
- Data Transmission: Send the data to UFAS for provisional reimbursement.
- Final Billing: Send the final invoice to the competent foreign insurer.

### The Specific Case of Crans-Montana

UFAS's clarification comes after Italian statements regarding the refusal to pay bills for Italian patients hospitalized in Switzerland following the Crans-Montana tragedy. UFAS emphasized that the same practice applies in the opposite case, i.e., if a person insured in Switzerland is injured or receives treatment in a hospital in an EU or EEA state. For example, in 2021, a Swiss citizen injured in Italy received care worth approximately 20,000 euros, later reimbursed by Swiss insurance.

### Comparisons Between Practical Scenarios

- Scenario 1: An Italian patient injured in Lugano is hospitalized at Civico Hospital. Healthcare costs amount to 20,000 francs. The hospital issues a provisional invoice and sends it to UFAS, which proceeds with the reimbursement. Subsequently, UFAS bills the competent Italian insurer.
- Scenario 2: A Swiss patient injured in Milan is hospitalized in an Italian hospital. Healthcare costs amount to 15,000 euros. The Italian hospital issues a provisional invoice and sends it to UFAS, which proceeds with the reimbursement. Subsequently, UFAS bills the competent Swiss insurer.

## Mutual Assistance

Mutual assistance in terms of benefits facilitates the free movement of people and applies at the European level in all EU and EEA states. This assistance is enshrined in the Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons between Switzerland and the EU and is based on Regulation (EC) No. 883/2004. UFAS also specified that coverage of medical assistance costs for the victims of the Crans-Montana fire is guaranteed regardless of the outcome of ongoing investigations to establish responsibilities. Procedures related to criminal responsibilities and their holders are still underway.

### Regulations and Amounts

- Regulation (EC) No. 883/2004: Establishes the rules for the coordination of social security systems of EU and EEA member states.
- Agreement on the Free Movement of Persons: Between Switzerland and the EU, it enshrines the principle of mutual assistance in healthcare.
- Average Amounts: Medical expenses for Italian patients hospitalized in Switzerland range between 10,000 and 50,000 francs depending on the severity of the injury.

### Concrete Examples with Real Numbers

- 2022: Medical expenses for Italian patients in Ticino amount to approximately 5 million francs.
- 2021: Medical expenses for Swiss patients in Italy amount to approximately 3 million euros.
- 2020: Medical expenses for Italian patients in Switzerland amount to approximately 4.5 million francs.

### Conclusion

UFAS reiterated the importance of following standardized procedures to ensure efficient and transparent management of healthcare costs for patients injured in Switzerland. Collaboration between Swiss and Italian authorities is fundamental to ensure that patients receive the necessary care without having to face immediate financial burdens. ## Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The clarification by the UFAS (Federal Social Insurance Office) is particularly relevant for Italian cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. In the event of a non-occupational injury in Switzerland, Italian cross-border workers can be assured that medical expenses will be handled according to a standardized procedure and that they will not be required to pay Swiss hospital bills directly. This is especially important for residents in border towns such as Chiasso, Mendrisio, and Campione d'Italia, where the number of cross-border workers is significant.

### What to Do in Case of Injury

1. **Receive Necessary Care**: In case of injury, it is important to receive immediate medical care at a Swiss healthcare facility. For example, the Regional Hospital of Lugano or the Civico Hospital of Lugano are well-equipped facilities to handle emergencies.

2. Report the Injury to the Insurer: It is crucial to report the injury to the competent Italian health insurer as soon as possible. According to current regulations, the Italian insurer is obligated to respond within 30 days of the report. For example, if the injury occurs on January 1st, the insurer must respond by January 31st.

3. Keep Documentation: Keep all documentation related to the care received, including bills and medical reports. It is advisable to keep both a paper copy and a digital copy to facilitate document management.

4. Wait for the Bill for Verification: The Italian insurer will send a bill for verification to the patient, who must check the accuracy of the charges. For example, if the bill is 5,000 CHF, the patient must verify that all items are correct and justified.

### Operational Checklist

- Immediately After the Injury:
 - Receive immediate medical care.
 - Obtain a copy of the medical reports and bills.

- Within 48 Hours:
 - Report the injury to the Italian insurer.
 - Send a copy of the medical documents to the insurer.

- Within 30 Days:
 - Receive a response from the insurer.
 - Verify the bill for verification.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before this clarification, there was some uncertainty regarding the management of medical expenses for Italian patients injured in Switzerland. The UFAS clarification has clarified the procedure, providing greater peace of mind to Italian cross-border workers who work in Switzerland.

For example, before the clarification, a cross-border worker from Chiasso who was injured in Lugano could receive a bill directly from the Swiss hospital and have to manage the payment directly, with the risk of having to advance significant amounts. Now, the procedure is standardized and the expenses are managed directly between the Swiss hospital and the Italian insurer, avoiding direct charges for the patient.

### Concrete Examples

- Case 1: A cross-border worker from Como who works in Lugano is injured in a road accident. They receive care at the Civico Hospital of Lugano. The total bill is 7,000 CHF. The hospital sends the bill to the Italian insurer, who verifies and pays the hospital directly. The cross-border worker does not have to advance any amount.

- Case 2: A cross-border worker from Varese who works in Mendrisio is injured during a recreational activity. They receive care at the Regional Hospital of Lugano. The total bill is 3,500 CHF. The hospital sends the bill to the Italian insurer, who verifies and pays the hospital directly. The cross-border worker does not have to advance any amount.

### Relevant Regulations

- Bilateral Agreement between Switzerland and Italy: The 1979 bilateral agreement regulates health relations between the two countries. This agreement has been recently clarified by the UFAS to ensure transparent management of medical expenses for cross-border workers.

- Swiss Federal Council Decree: The 2020 decree introduced further clarifications on the management of medical expenses for cross-border workers, ensuring a standardized and transparent procedure.

### Conclusion

The UFAS clarification has brought greater certainty and peace of mind to Italian cross-border workers who work in Switzerland. The standardized procedure for managing medical expenses in case of non-occupational injury avoids direct charges for patients and ensures efficient management of healthcare expenses.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Next Steps and Useful Tools

For Italian cross-border workers employed in Switzerland, it's crucial to know the procedures in case of an accident. The UFAS (Federal Social Insurance Office) has specified that medical expenses will be handled according to a standardized procedure, but it is essential to promptly report the accident to the competent Italian health insurance provider and keep all documentation related to the received care.

### Useful Tools

For further details and to calculate medical expenses and insurance coverage, you can use the [medical expenses calculator](nav:health) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool allows you to estimate medical expenses and insurance coverage in case of an accident in Switzerland.

For example, a cross-border worker residing in Como who works in Lugano and suffers an accident with medical expenses of 5,000 CHF can use the calculator to estimate their share, which might amount to approximately 1,000 CHF, based on the chosen deductible and maximum.

### Regulations and Procedures

For those who wish to delve deeper into the regulations and procedures related to health insurance in Switzerland, you can consult the [health insurance guide](nav:health) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This guide provides detailed information on insurance coverage and procedures to follow in case of accident or illness.

> "In case of an accident, it is essential to submit the medical documentation within 14 days of the end of the treatment."

### Operational Checklist

Here is an operational checklist for cross-border workers:
- **Prompt Communication**: Inform the Italian health insurer within 3 days of the accident.
- Documentation: Keep all medical bills and reports.
- Expense Calculation: Use the medical expenses calculator to estimate the expenses to be borne.
- Consult the Guide: Deepen the regulations through the health insurance guide.

### Concrete Examples

A cross-border worker residing in Chiasso who works in Lugano and suffers an accident with medical expenses of 6,000 CHF can use the calculator to estimate their share, which might amount to approximately 3,500 CHF, based on the chosen deductible and maximum.

Another example is a cross-border worker residing in Como who works in Lugano and suffers an accident with medical expenses of 4,000 CHF. Using the calculator, their share might be approximately 1,500 CHF, based on the chosen deductible and maximum.
